frameworks.name: mnemonic metacognition
frameworks.one_line: evaluation of one's own memory performance as a measurable cognitive faculty

frameworks.name: virtual-lesion rTMS
frameworks.one_line: inhibitory repetitive TMS as causal probe of precuneal function

frameworks.name: individual-differences neuroimaging
frameworks.one_line: anatomical and rs-fc profiles modulate susceptibility to TMS perturbation

core_question: How do TMS-induced neuromodulatory effects on precuneal metamemory relate to neural correlates and individual anatomical profiles?
key_assumptions: precuneus causally supports metamemory upon recollection
individual MRI neuro-profiles index TMS susceptibility
vertex stimulation is an adequate control site
BOLD magnitude indexes underlying metamemory computation
manipulations: individual neuro-profiles (rs-fc, grey matter volume, task-fMRI)
key_findings: precuneal BOLD magnitude correlates with individual metamemory efficiency
precuneal TMS significantly diminishes that BOLD-metamemory correlation
higher precuneus-hippocampus rs-fc predicts greater TMS vulnerability
smaller grey matter volume in stimulated precuneus predicts greater TMS vulnerability
interpretations: precuneal contribution to metamemory is causally affirmed and individually graded
effect is memory-domain specific, not generic to perceptual judgements
